title: Vital XP
description: A survival overhaul mod where health is tied to experience. Easily configurable.
body:
**Description:**

Vital XP adds a **progression system** to Minecraft by linking a player’s **maximum health** to their **experience level**. 

Instead of having a fixed amount of hearts, players **gain health as they level up**, making XP a core survival mechanic rather than a simple enchanting resource.

**How does it work?**
- Players start with a minimum, configurable, amount of health.
- Health is capped at a configurable maximum.
- Every few XP levels, the player gains **additional hearts**.
- Losing XP **reduces maximum health**.

> Once the maximum health cap is reached, any extra health gained is **not wasted**:

- Extra health becomes **permanently locked hearts**.
- **Locked hearts** are **never lost**, even if **XP decreases**.
- If the player dies, they lose their locked hearts if they have any.

**Configuration:**

All values can be adjusted in-game using commands or the configuration.

**For versions below 1.21.1:** type “/vitalxp help” to display the commands.

**For versions 1.21.1 and above:** the “/vitalxp” commands are no longer available. To configure the mod, go to the mods menu, select the mod, and press the “Config” button.
